title: The Art of Layering Festive Dupattas
excerpt: From drape to tassel, here is how we style a dupatta to elevate any festive look without overwhelming it.
content:
A dupatta is more than an accessory — it is the finishing sentence of an outfit. At KINDRED, we choose dupatta fabrics that contrast gently with the base garment: a matte silk dress calls for a sheer net dupatta, while a heavily embroidered piece is best paired with something quieter.

Our styling team suggests draping the dupatta over one shoulder and letting it fall asymmetrically rather than centering it — this creates movement and keeps the silhouette modern. For evening events, pin the drape so it stays in place through the night.

Finally, never underestimate the tassel. A small detail at the end of a dupatta or sash adds a tactile, handcrafted feel that photographs beautifully and feels luxurious in person.

title: Caring for Your Lace and Silk Pieces
excerpt: Lace and silk are investment fabrics. A few simple habits will keep your KINDRED pieces looking new for years.
content:
Lace overlays and silk blends are at the heart of many KINDRED designs, and both fabrics reward a little extra care. Always dry clean rather than machine wash — water and friction can pull delicate lace threads and dull silk's natural sheen.

Store your dresses on padded hangers, never folded, to avoid permanent creasing along the bodice. If you must travel with a piece, roll it loosely in acid-free tissue paper rather than folding.

Lastly, keep your garments out of direct sunlight when not in use. UV exposure is the single biggest cause of fading in rich, saturated colors like deep maroon and amethyst.

title: Our New Season Color Story
excerpt: Soft ivory, warm terracotta, and deep amethyst lead our new collection. Here's the thinking behind the palette.
content:
Every KINDRED season begins with a color story before a single sketch is drawn. This season, we were drawn to the warmth of sun-baked terracotta walls and the quiet elegance of ivory organza — tones that feel equally at home in daylight and candlelight.

We balanced those warm neutrals with a single saturated accent: a deep amethyst purple that shows up in our festive sets. The result is a collection that mixes effortlessly, whether you are building a capsule wardrobe or shopping for one statement piece.

Expect to see this palette carried through not just in our dresses, but in our packaging, our journal, and the way we shoot our new arrivals.
